conclusion: Grouped_compact MoE plus the accepted c2/c4/c8 projection-dispatch catalog preserves generated-token equality for c2/c4/c8 512/128 vs independent c1. Each row count reports moe_decode_path=grouped_compact, moe_grouped_compact_layers=40, moe_selected_c1_fallback_layers=0, and a c-aware projection candidate with empty projection blockers. No throughput claim is made: profiler evidence for this grouped path is not captured here, c4/c8 still use rowchunked full attention/native_caware_decode=false, and c8 repeatability remains an open blocker.
remaining_blockers: profiler evidence is not captured for the grouped_compact+projection-dispatch path
c4/c8 full attention still uses native_batch_row_chunks and native_caware_decode=false
c8 grouped_compact+projection-dispatch equality was unstable before the final green rerun
retained performance claims still require docs/BENCHMARK.md gates and profiler/scaling acceptance
repeatability_note: The c8 grouped_compact+projection-dispatch command was red on the first retained run in this iteration and green on the immediate rerun that is retained here; this matches prior c8 projection-dispatch instability. Treat c8 repeatability as an open blocker before any promotion/performance claim.

4: native c>N decode currently supports compact physical-slot-ordered rows; full-attention batch context is native only for BF16 KV and context < 1024
full-attention decode forced to native row-chunk diagnostic path
8: native c>N decode currently supports compact physical-slot-ordered rows; full-attention batch context is native only for BF16 KV and context < 1024
